nodes.label: MIPROv2 prompt optimization pattern
nodes.rationale: Uses DSPy MIPROv2 with auto=light and no bootstrapped/labeled demos to perform lightweight prompt optimization; post-optimization placeholder restoration ensures template variables survive rewriting.

nodes.label: Local vs Synced Settings Split
nodes.rationale: Sensitive/device-specific settings (iclaudePath, apiKey, proxy, backend) live in local.json (not synced). Plugin settings (history, timeouts, systemPrompt) sync via Obsidian data.json. migrateToLocalV1 moves per-device fields out of data.json.

nodes.label: obsidian-llm-wiki Plugin
nodes.rationale: Obsidian plugin that performs wiki operations (ingest/query/lint/fix/init/chat) via TypeScript phases with LLM backend, displaying progress in a sidebar in real time.

nodes.label: WikiController
nodes.rationale: Implements single-flight guard and path validation (cwd/iclaudePath). Rejects parallel runs via this._running to prevent stdout stream corruption.

nodes.label: AgentRunner
nodes.rationale: Routes operations to the appropriate TypeScript phase, handles dev logging and evaluation.

nodes.label: ClaudeCliClient
nodes.rationale: Spawns iclaude.sh process, provides OpenAI-compatible LlmClient interface. Manages stdio with stdin closed, stdout/stderr captured. Handles SIGTERM -> 3000ms grace -> SIGKILL abort sequence.

nodes.label: parseStreamLine
nodes.rationale: Parses a single JSON line from iclaude stdout into a RunEvent. Non-JSON lines (iclaude banners) are ignored.

nodes.label: LlmWikiView
nodes.rationale: Obsidian ItemView subclass that renders steps, metrics, and history live in the sidebar panel.

nodes.label: main.ts
nodes.rationale: Plugin entry point; registers commands, view, and settings. Also the esbuild entrypoint producing main.js (CJS, ES2022).

nodes.label: settings.ts
nodes.rationale: Plugin settings and autodetectCwd() which walks up the directory tree up to 6 levels.

nodes.label: types.ts
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for all TypeScript types: WikiOperation, RunEvent, LlmWikiPluginSettings, WikiDomain. WikiDomain union type on line 8 is the canonical domain registry.

nodes.label: WikiOperation
nodes.rationale: Enum/union type representing the supported wiki operations: ingest, query, lint, fix, init, chat. Each has its own timeout setting.

nodes.label: RunEvent
nodes.rationale: Typed representation of a parsed stream-json line from iclaude stdout. Variants: system/init, system/error, assistant, user/tool_result, result.

nodes.label: WikiDomain
nodes.rationale: Union type 'ии' | 'ростелеком' | 'базы-данных'. Single source of truth for all domain values — only this type should be modified when adding a domain.

nodes.label: stream-json protocol
nodes.rationale: Each stdout line from iclaude is one JSON object. Types: system (init|error), assistant (tool_use|text), user (tool_result), result (duration_ms, total_cost_usd, is_error). Non-JSON lines are ignored.

nodes.label: single-flight guard
nodes.rationale: Only one operation runs at a time. controller.ts rejects parallel launches via this._running. Parallel spawning would corrupt the stdout stream. Other callers receive an Obsidian Notice.

nodes.label: iclaude.sh
nodes.rationale: Shell wrapper spawned by ClaudeCliClient. Reserves -p/--proxy for proxy URL. Flags must be passed as: iclaude.sh flags (--no-proxy, --model) then -- then claude flags (-p <prompt>, --output-format). Violating this causes exit 1 with no stderr.

nodes.label: historyLimit
nodes.rationale: Default 20. History stored in Obsidian settings. Exceeding the limit slows Obsidian's settings.json save. The value is a UX vs. settings.json size tradeoff.

nodes.label: versioning workflow
nodes.rationale: Before every build: auto-increment patch version in package.json and src/manifest.json, then run npm run build. Minor and major bumps are manual only.

nodes.label: esbuild.config.mjs
nodes.rationale: Build config: entrypoint src/main.ts -> main.js (CJS, ES2022). Externals: obsidian, electron, node:child_process, node:readline. path-browserify is bundled; node:path and node:fs are removed from externals.

nodes.label: tests/stream.test.ts
nodes.rationale: Tests parseStreamLine() with fixture JSONL.

nodes.label: tests/settings.test.ts
nodes.rationale: Tests autodetectCwd() directory walk-up behavior.

nodes.label: tests/claude-cli-client.test.ts
nodes.rationale: Tests ClaudeCliClient: streaming, abort, large payload, session resume.

nodes.label: tests/agent-runner.integration.test.ts
nodes.rationale: Integration tests for AgentRunner with a mock adapter.

nodes.label: tests/fixtures/stream-ingest.jsonl
nodes.rationale: Reference JSONL fixture for stream tests.

nodes.label: tests/fixtures/mock-iclaude.sh
nodes.rationale: Bash mock that replays JSONL with delay, simulating iclaude.sh in tests.

nodes.label: vitest.mock.ts
nodes.rationale: Obsidian API mocks, loaded automatically via vitest.config.ts.

nodes.label: Prompt placeholder syntax: {{variable}} for runtime template substitution
nodes.norm_label: prompt placeholder syntax: {{variable}} for runtime template substitution

nodes.label: wiki_* frontmatter fields preserved on format apply — programmatically managed, must not be lost during LLM reformatting
nodes.norm_label: wiki_* frontmatter fields preserved on format apply — programmatically managed, must not be lost during llm reformatting

nodes.label: Community Plugin Submission Process
nodes.rationale: Plugins are pulled by Obsidian reading community-plugins.json; manifest.json determines latest version; actual files fetched from GitHub releases tagged to match manifest version. This ensures reproducibility and version compatibility checking via versions.json.

nodes.label: Dynalist, Inc. (Obsidian)
nodes.rationale: Legal entity managing Obsidian. Holds copyright license grants from contributors via CLA. Not open source but accepts community plugins and themes.

nodes.label: CLA Copyright License Grant
nodes.rationale: Perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, irrevocable copyright license. Contributors retain ownership of their work but grant Obsidian right to reproduce, prepare derivatives, display, perform, sublicense, and distribute contributions.

nodes.label: CLA Patent License Grant
nodes.rationale: Perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free patent license for patent claims that are necessarily infringed by the contribution alone or in combination with the work. Revocable only if patent litigation is initiated.

nodes.label: {{placeholder}} template syntax
nodes.rationale: Templates use {{placeholder}} syntax so that restore_placeholders() can ensure named slots survive MIPROv2 rewriting, preserving prompt structure through optimization.

nodes.label: DSPy MIPROv2: automated prompt optimization via Optuna
nodes.norm_label: dspy miprov2: automated prompt optimization via optuna

nodes.label: ClaudeCodeLM: DSPy-compatible adapter for Claude CLI
nodes.norm_label: claudecodelm: dspy-compatible adapter for claude cli

nodes.label: Obsidian Graph View UI — Light Theme Screenshot
nodes.rationale: Shows Obsidian October 2021 event page alongside the Graph View panel in light mode. Left sidebar shows file tree (Obsidian Hub), center panel shows an interactive force-directed knowledge graph with node clusters and color-coded groups, right panel shows the October 2021 event article. Demonstrates Obsidian's core graph visualization feature and community engagement via official events.
nodes.norm_label: obsidian graph view ui — light theme screenshot

nodes.label: Obsidian Graph View
nodes.rationale: Force-directed interactive graph visualization panel built into Obsidian. Supports grouping nodes by color, filtering, and showing forces between linked notes. Core UX feature visible in both light and dark screenshots.

nodes.label: Obsidian Graph View UI — Dark Theme Screenshot
nodes.rationale: Same Obsidian October 2021 event page layout in dark mode. Confirms plugin/theme dark mode support requirement. Graph nodes appear as bright dots on dark background with the same force-directed layout. Shows Obsidian's dual light/dark theme capability that community themes must support.
nodes.norm_label: obsidian graph view ui — dark theme screenshot
